metadata.description: Metering and cost tracking attached to compute resources managed by other providers. It provisions VISIBILITY (metering, overhead distribution, financial tracking), not infrastructure — a long-running observational Process. VENDOR-NEUTRAL by requirement: rates are expressed over ABSTRACT cost dimensions (compute_time, memory_time, ...), never a provider's native metric names; any compliant cost provider maps each abstract dimension to its own native metric in its Provider Catalog Item — NOT in this type. UDLM-correct translation of the `cost` serviceType in dcm-project/enhancements#60: this type carries the cost MODEL (the rate table over portable dimensions — declarative data); the provider naturalizes those dimensions, COMPUTES the cost (metering x rate, overhead distribution — Policy), and denaturalizes the result into the typed Realized outputs. The metered target is a typed relationship resolved on the instance, not an id baked into the type spec.
metadata.portability_notes: Fully portable: every field is vendor-neutral. Provider-specific cost vocabulary (native metric names, provider cost-type taxonomies) is deliberately NOT carried here — it belongs to the Provider Catalog Item (the E1 offering layer), which maps each abstract `dimension` to its native metric. Rationale + the dcm#60 comparison: docs/resource-type-registry-design-notes.md §4a.

target.description: What kind of resource this meter tracks. The SPECIFIC resource(s) are bound on the instance via the `references` relationship (the realized edge carries the target uuid) — the type carries only the declarative type selector, never an instance id.

resourceType.description: Resource Type name of the metered target (dcm#60 `target.resource_type`).

costModel.description: Rate configuration. ABSENT = basic metering only (tier 1 — usage flows, no cost). PRESENT without `rates` = distribution only (tier 2 — overhead categorized). PRESENT with `rates` = full cost (tier 3). Tier is derived purely from what is present (E3, presence-conditional) — no expression language; `cost = metering x rate` is the provider's act (Policy), not carried here.

rates.description: Rate definitions over ABSTRACT, vendor-neutral cost dimensions. Each rate is declarative data; mapping a dimension to a provider's native metric, and applying the rate to observed usage, is the provider's job (Policy / its Catalog Item).

dimension.description: Portable cost dimension (aligned to FOCUS, the FinOps Open Cost & Usage Specification). The provider naturalizes this to its own native metric in its Catalog Item — that mapping is provider-specific and lives there, not here.

value.description: Rate value per unit of the dimension, in `currency` (>= 0).

per.description: Billing period the rate value is expressed against.

category.description: Universal cost-attribution intent: `direct` = directly attributable to the metered resource; `overhead` = shared/allocated cost. Captures the INTENT behind any provider cost-type taxonomy in vendor-neutral terms; the provider naturalizes its native categories to these two concepts.

distribution.description: Portable allocation basis for distributing shared overhead across metered resources.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for rate values and computed cost. Defaults to USD when omitted; required once `rates` are present (E3).

computedCost.description: Cost for the period = metering x rate (+ markup), present only at tier 3. Output of the provider's computation; the formula itself is Policy and is NOT carried in the spec (G2).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency of `computedCost` (echoes the realized spec value).

meteredUsage.description: Per-dimension observed usage (tier 1+): items of { dimension, quantity, unit } over the portable dimension vocabulary — typed + vendor-neutral, not a provider-shaped blob.

costBreakdown.description: Per-dimension cost after distribution (tier 2+): items of { dimension, amount }. Vendor-neutral binding surface.

relationships.description: Meters a cluster. Cross-cutting reference (applies-to), not a lifecycle dependency — the target's lifecycle is independent (T4). At least one target across all reference edges is required at instance time (enforced by Policy).
